note: Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Covering/Differentiation.lean:867 — 'Lebesgue differentiation theorem: for a.e. x, average of ‖f y - f x‖ on a tends to 0 as a shrinks to x along a Vitali family.' This is exactly the a.e.-every-point-is-a-Lebesgue-point statement, but generalized to abstract Vitali families/metric measure spaces rather than a named 'Lebesgue point' predicate on ℝⁿ.
